texto: God enjoys himself, kills, commits injustice, makes love, works, likes impossible things, just the same as I do.But, boss, I´ve said so before, and I say it again, God and the devil are one and the same thing!

texto: Christ is born, my wise Solomon, my wretched pen-pusher! Don´t go picking things over with a needle! Is He born or isn´t He? Of course He is born, don´t be daft. If you take a magnifying glass and look at your drinking water-an engineer told me this, one day – you´ll see, he said, the water´s full of little worms you couldn´t see with your naked eye. You´ll see the worms and you won´t drink. You won´t drink and you´ll curl up with thirst. Smash your glass, boss, and the little worms´ll vanish and you can drink and be refreshed!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Nikos Kazantsakis que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Christ is born, my wise Solomon, my wretched pen-pusher! Don´t go picking things over with a need…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, christ, worms, com foco em reflexao.

texto: What a mystery woman is, boss! Even if she falls thousand times, she rises a thousand times a virgin. But how´s that? you´ll say. Because she doesn´t remember!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Nikos Kazantsakis que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What a mystery woman is, boss! Even if she falls thousand times, she rises a thousand times a vir…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre tempo, mystery, virgin, com foco em virgin e woman.
